findings.detail: Read docs/catalog/heartbeats/2026-05-21T05-06-45Z-atlas.json — gi=0.804 mode=green source=kv; 12 anomalies (elevated third-party lanes); seed_result="200 OK"; sustain_eligible=false; kv_keys show core lanes present including GI_STATE, SIGNAL_SNAPSHOT, VAULT_*, MIC_*, TRIPWIRE_*.

GET /api/signals/micro 200 — composite=0.644 gi=0.644 errors=10 instrumentCount=40; each ATLAS-flagged lane still elevated or error-sourced in live instruments. Micro payload cycle=C-306 vs integrity/CURRENT_CYCLE C-318 (metadata drift). Live also shows NASA APIs health elevated though not in the catalog heartbeat anomaly list (catalog vs sweep timing skew).

GET /api/integrity-status 200 — source=kv (not mock); global_integrity=0.779 mode=yellow terminal_status=stressed degraded=true; gi_verified=false gi_provenance=live-compute. Cross-check: headline GI above micro composite but below ATLAS catalog snapshot — lane-dependent aggregation.
GET /api/epicon/candidates 200 — count 0; no candidates, verification skipped (no POST /api/zeus/verify or /api/epicon/publish).
POST /api/vault/attest HTTP 404 (Next.js not-found); quorum_status not returned.
POST /api/agents/journal HTTP 502 — ok=false error="ledger 400: No API base configured for terminal" mirrored_to_kv=false.

recommendation: Third-party elevated lanes are persistent in live micro data; KV integrity is live (not mock) so GI disagreement is aggregation/cycle metadata, not seed failure. Deploy POST /api/vault/attest for C-298 quorum registration. Configure terminal ledger API base so agent journal writes succeed. Reconcile micro sweep cycle field with CURRENT_CYCLE.
